title: Transmission Station Threat Assessment Evidence Indexing

compliance.assurance_rationale: Uplifted from contributing to partial: UC provides meaningful monitoring evidence for CIP-014-3 R1 Part 1.1.
compliance.controlObjective: Evidence that NERC CIP CIP-014-3 R1 Part 1.1 is enforced — Splunk UC-22.13.68: Transmission Station Threat Assessment Evidence Indexing.
compliance.evidenceArtifact: Saved search 'UC-22.13.68' running on index=grc sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment, archived to the restricted audit_evidence index (default 7-year retention). Auto-drafted — SME review required.

spl:
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment earliest=-730d
| eval completed=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval due_review=relative_time(completed,"+3y@d")
| where now()>due_review
| table station_id, completed_date, reviewer, due_review, next_assessment_scheduled
description: We index completed threat assessment metadata for transmission stations so evidence dates and reviewers are searchable for CIP-014 reviews.
value: Gives the responsible team auditor-ready evidence for NERC CIP compliance by automating regulatory compliance evidence using indexes grc and sourcetypes cip014_threat_assessment, cip014_threat_assessment`. Replaces manual evidence gathering with a continuous Splunk search that produces structured, time-stamped artefacts suitable for regulatory examination and internal audit review.
implementation: (1) Push assessment completions via HEC when signed. (2) Set review cadence per program (example three years). (3) Alert owners before due. (4) Attach PDF hash externally if required. Indexes required: `index=grc`; Sourcetypes: `sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment`, `sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment``.
visualization: Table (stations due), Timeline, Single value (count). Key fields: `completed`, `completed_date`, `due_review`, `next_assessment_scheduled`, `reviewer`, `station_id`.
knownFalsePositives: Planned maintenance, registered change tickets, and approved work can look like noncompliance in raw telemetry; confirm against the current change record before you escalate.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Splunk Enterprise Security (263).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
(1) Push assessment completions via HEC when signed. (2) Set review cadence per program (example three years). (3) Alert owners before due. (4) Attach PDF hash externally if required.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=grc sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment earliest=-730d
| eval completed=strptime(completed_date,"%Y-%m-%d")
| eval due_review=relative_time(completed,"+3y@d")
| where now()>due_review
| table station_id, completed_date, reviewer, due_review, next_assessment_scheduled
```

Understanding this SPL

**Transmission Station Threat Assessment Evidence Indexing (CIP-014-3 R1 Part 1.1)** — We index completed threat assessment metadata for transmission stations so evidence dates and reviewers are searchable for CIP-014 reviews.

Documented **Data sources**: `index=grc` `sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Splunk Enterprise Security (263). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: grc; **sourcetype**: cip014_threat_assessment. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=grc, sourcetype=cip014_threat_assessment, time bounds. Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **completed** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **due_review** — applying business logic to classify, threshold, or derive compliance-relevant indicators.
• Filters the current rows with `where now()>due_review` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.

Output fields: completed, completed_date, due_review, next_assessment_scheduled, reviewer, station_id.

Step 3 — Validate
Compare with the ESP firewall log, Windows Security, or the asset register—whichever the search is built to mirror. Spot-check a few source events, reconcile counts, and verify field extractions, thresholds, and index permissions for the people who need this evidence.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Table (stations due), Timeline, Single value (count)
grandmaExplanation: We index completed threat assessment metadata for transmission stations so evidence dates and reviewers are searchable for CIP-014 reviews. That is important for the big power grid, where proof needs to line up with what the rules expect.
